question: What is the maximum allowable HIC value under §571.208?

chunks.text: Head injury criteria. (a)(1) For any two points in time, t1 and t2, during the event which are separated by not more than a 36 millisecond time interval and where t1 is less than t2, the head injury criterion (HIC36) shall be determined using the resultant head acceleration at the center of gravity of the dummy head, ar, expressed as a multiple of g (the acceleration of gravity) and shall be calculated using the expression: (2) The maximum calculated HIC36 value shall not exceed 1,000. (b) (1) For any two points in time, t1 and t2, during the event which are separated by not more than a 15 millisecond time interval and where t1 is less than t2, the head injury criterion (HIC15) shall be determined using the resultant head acceleration at the center of gravity of the dummy head, ar, expressed as a multiple of g (the acceleration of gravity) and shall be calculated using the expression: (2) The maximum calculated HIC15 value shall not exceed 700.
chunks.parent_heading: Injury criteria for the part 572, subpart E, Hybrid III test dummy.

question: What are the maximum values for both head injury criterion and chest deflection under §571.208?

chunks.text: Head injury criteria. (a)(1) For any two points in time, t1 and t2, during the event which are separated by not more than a 36 millisecond time interval and where t1 is less than t2, the head injury criterion (HIC36) shall be determined using the resultant head acceleration at the center of gravity of the dummy head, ar, expressed as a multiple of g (the acceleration of gravity) and shall be calculated using the expression: (2) The maximum calculated HIC36 value shall not exceed 1,000. (b) (1) For any two points in time, t1 and t2, during the event which are separated by not more than a 15 millisecond time interval and where t1 is less than t2, the head injury criterion (HIC15) shall be determined using the resultant head acceleration at the center of gravity of the dummy head, ar, expressed as a multiple of g (the acceleration of gravity) and shall be calculated using the expression: (2) The maximum calculated HIC15 value shall not exceed 700.
chunks.parent_heading: Injury criteria for the part 572, subpart E, Hybrid III test dummy.

chunks.text: Chest deflection. (a) Compressive deflection of the sternum relative to the spine shall not exceed 76 mm (3.0 in). (b) Compressive deflection of the sternum relative to the spine shall not exceed 63 mm (2.5 in).
chunks.parent_heading: Injury criteria for the part 572, subpart E, Hybrid III test dummy.

question: What is the maximum axial force allowed through each femur for the 5th-percentile adult female dummy under §571.208?

chunks.text: The force transmitted axially through each femur shall not exceed 6805 N (1530 lb).
chunks.parent_heading: Rigid barrier test requirements using 5th percentile adult female dummies. — Injury criteria for the 49 CFR part 572, subpart O Hybrid III 5th percentile female test dummy.

question: Cite the §571.208 clause requiring biometric driver authentication before airbag arming.

chunks.text: Chest deflection. (a) Compressive deflection of the sternum relative to the spine shall not exceed 76 mm (3.0 in). (b) Compressive deflection of the sternum relative to the spine shall not exceed 63 mm (2.5 in).
chunks.parent_heading: Injury criteria for the part 572, subpart E, Hybrid III test dummy.

question: What is the maximum allowable chest deflection under §571.208?

chunks.text: Chest deflection. (a) Compressive deflection of the sternum relative to the spine shall not exceed 76 mm (3.0 in). (b) Compressive deflection of the sternum relative to the spine shall not exceed 63 mm (2.5 in).
chunks.parent_heading: Injury criteria for the part 572, subpart E, Hybrid III test dummy.

question: What is the maximum resultant thoracic (chest) acceleration allowed under §571.208, and is there any exception?

chunks.text: The resultant acceleration calculated from the output of the thoracic instrumentation shown in drawing 78051.218, revision R incorporated by reference in part 572, subpart E of this chapter shall not exceed 60 g's, except for intervals whose cumulative duration is not more than 3 milliseconds.
chunks.parent_heading: Injury criteria for the part 572, subpart E, Hybrid III test dummy.
